title: Adventurous!
description: optimizations, worldgen, and more, all in one!
body:
Adventurous! is a modpack primarily featuring, alongside the standard suite of optimization mods (because who wants to have to edit a modpack right out of the box?), lots of worldgen mods. 
Vanilla biomes and structures have been tweaked or completely overhauled, and lots of modded new biomes and structures have been added. The Nether and End have gotten a complete overhaul, and several new dimensions have been added on top of that. One of them is designed to be kind of a "secret surprise", so I'd recommend just creating a new world and trying it out, without looking through the mod list. Or don't, I can't stop you.  
I have also included lots of quality-of-life tweaks as I saw fit, and a few mods that I just honestly found funny (who doesn't want to be able to put your pants in the head slot?). 
Overall, this pack is designed to be a plug-and-play system for anyone who wants to get into modded minecraft that still mostly feels "vanilla." This pack is perfect for someone who has previously played mostly vanilla, or has transferred over from the Optifine community in search of better fps and feature customization.  
The one thing I haven't added much of is the Create-style technical progression. To be completely honest, it kind of scares me. I've added plenty of vanilla progression options to balance this out. 

There are a few issues and unwanted behavior that I've experienced, but for the most part it's small things that can be resolved with a bit of ironing out. I don't have any expectations that people will play this pack, but if you do, community feedback is the most valuable reward I get. If there's a feature or mod you're dying to see in this pack, feel free to request it. I'll do my best. 

Beta 1.1 does not support multiplayer, but the most recent version should. 

Screenshots are forthcoming! 

Due to the permissions set forth by some of the mod authors, please do not rehost this pack outside of Modrinth


All mods in this pack are available on Modrinth except for Liroth and More Villagers, which are available on Curseforge, linked below, and the Mobhead datapacks, which are from VanillaTweaks  
Please note that there are over 300, going on 400 mods in this pack. Once the libraries are fully expanded the number is more like 500. Optimization mods can only do so much, so it will take a bit longer to launch your game. Not much I can do about it unfortunately.  
Full mod list below:

[Saturated](https://modrinth.com/mod/saturated) This is the "secret" dimension I mentioned earlier. To get there, get crushed by falling gravel or sand. To get to different sub-dimensions, go through a warped or iron door. To return to the overworld, get crushed by gravel/sand again.

[Sparse Structures](https://modrinth.com/mod/sparsestructures) disabled by default. This one is a matter of personal preference. The dev of the mod is looking into making it an option in the worldgen menu to change the "Generate Structures" toggle from a simple on/off switch into a off/sparse/regular/extreme type of thing. When that update releases, this mod can be left on all the time no matter your personal preferences.

[Various Starting Loot](https://modrinth.com/mod/various-starting-loot) disabled by default. Because this is a datapack packaged as a mod (for convenience), it generates a new biome-specific bonus chest every time you relog. Not much can be done about that, but it's fun to have once or twice when starting a new world.
